title: International trade and public policy
description: Practice what you have learned about the impact of trade, tariffs, and quotas on consumer and producer surplus in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does it mean to say that a country is “under autarky”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It does not engage in international trade
It imposes tariffs on imported goods
It imposes tariffs on exported goods
It imposes import quotas
It has no restrictions on international trade

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the domestic market for cheese with the world price of cheese.


![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/59d07f24cde965c658afd219b1b65a5b73035dc6)

**How much is imported or exported in this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $Q_c - Q_a$ is imported
$Q_c - Q_b$ is imported
$Q_c - Q_a$ is exported
$Q_a - Q_b$ is exported
$Q_b-Q_a$ is imported

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shown here illustrates the domestic market for cheese. The domestic price of cheese is $\$18$ and the world price of cheese is $\$10$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6dbdd57bdb352162484a165d8f091d379818620e)

**How much is imported or exported in this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: import $24$ tons of cheese
import $8$ tons of cheese
export $8$ tons of cheese
import $16$ tons of cheese
export $32$ tons of cheese

assessment_items.question_content:
The price of potatoes in Maxistan is $\$2$ per pound. The world price of potatoes is $\$3$ per pound.

**What will happen if Maxistan opens its economy to trade with other countries?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The price of potatoes in Maxistan increases and Maxistan becomes an exporter of potatoes.
The price of potatoes in Maxistan decreases and Maxistan becomes an importer of potatoes.
The price of potatoes in Maxistan increases and Maxistan becomes an importer of potatoes.
The price of potatoes in Maxistan decreases and Maxistan becomes an exporter of potatoes.
There is no change to the price of potatoes in Maxistan and Maxistan becomes an exporter of potatoes.

assessment_items.question_content:
The market for hiking backpacks in the nation of Montrose is shown in this graph. The world price of hiking backpacks is $\$20$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9fb214a517f3aec923df4b129296be581fde4b72)

**What is true if Montrose opens itself to trade?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The new equilibrium price is $\$20$ and Montrose will import $75$ backpacks
The equilibrium price is $\$20$ and $50$ backpacks are imported.
The equilibrium price is $\$20$ and $25$ backpacks are exported.
The equilibrium price is $\$70$ and $25$ backpacks are exported.
The equilibrium price cannot be determined but $50$ units are imported.

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume all costs and benefits are reflected in the supply and demand curves in a market, and neither curve is perfectly elastic or inelastic.

**What happens when a quota is placed on a market in an importing country?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Consumer surplus decreases, producer surplus increases, and total surplus decreases.
Consumer surplus and producer surplus both increase and deadweight loss is eliminated.
Consumer surplus and producer surplus both decrease and deadweight loss is eliminated.
Consumer surplus increases, producer surplus increases, deadweight loss exists, and tax revenue is generated.
Consumer surplus increases, producer surplus increases, and deadweight loss exists.

assessment_items.question_content:
**All of the following are true about quotas EXCEPT which option?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They generate tax revenue.
Consumer surplus decreases when a quota is imposed.
Producer surplus for license holders increases when a quota is imposed.
They cause deadweight loss.
Total welfare is lower with quotas than with free trade.
A tax on the import or export of a good
A limit on the quantity of a good that can be imported or exported.
The legal authority granted by a government to import and export goods.
A tax on goods produced and sold within a country.
Laws which determine where a product is from

assessment_items.question_content:
**What area(s) in this graph represents the tax revenue that is collected by a government when a tariff is imposed on a market?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3730df5f00625c47997afd6429f122ad39fe581a)

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $H+I$
$G+J$
$D+C $
$E+F$

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the domestic market for canned ham. A tariff has been placed on each can of ham imported. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/759adeadd98e5c438d77a6d0913defa629457366)

**What area(s) in this graph represents the consumer surplus that is reallocated away from consumers and to producers when a tariff is imposed in on this good?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $E+F$
$G+J$
$A+B+E+F$
$H+I$

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that demand and supply curves are neither perfectly elastic or perfectly inelastic.

**What are the effects of tariffs in an importing country?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Consumer surplus decreases, producer surplus increases, tax revenue is generated, and total surplus decreases.
Consumer surplus increases, producer surplus increases, and total surplus increases.
Consumer surplus decreases, producer surplus increases, and total surplus increases.
Consumer surplus increases, producer surplus decreases, and total surplus decreases.
Consumer surplus is unchanged, producer surplus increases, and total surplus increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the effect of a tariff?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It increases the domestic price of a good and decreases total surplus.
It decreases the domestic price of a good and has no effect on total surplus.
It increases the quantity of a good bought in a country and increases tax revenue
It decreases the quantity of a good bought in a country and decreases tax revenue.
It alters the behavior of buyers but not sellers.
